collection.collectionName: San Diego Natural History Museum Entomology Department
collection.additionalInfo: San Diego Natural History Museum Entomology Department (SDNHM-SDNHM)

abstract.para: This collection is a snapshot of an external collection database where only the Bee (Apoidea:Anthophila) records are included.

collection.collectionName: Essig Museum of Entomology, University of California Berkeley
collection.additionalInfo: Essig Museum of Entomology, University of California Berkeley (UCB-UCB)

abstract.para: This collection is a snapshot of an external collection database where only the Bee (Apoidea:Anthophila) records are included.

collection.collectionName: Museum of Comparative Zoology, Harvard University
collection.additionalInfo: President and Fellows of Harvard College

abstract.para: This collection is a snapshot of an external collection database where only the Bee (Apoidea:Anthophila) records are included.

collection.collectionName: University of Kansas Natural History Museum Entomology Division
collection.additionalInfo: University of Kansas Natural History Museum Entomology Division (KU-KU)

abstract.para: This collection is a snapshot of an external collection database where only the Bee (Apoidea:Anthophila) records are included.

collection.collectionName: University of Colorado Museum of Natural History, Entomology Collection
collection.additionalInfo: University of Colorado Museum of Natural History, Entomology Collection (UCM-UCM)

abstract.para: This collection is a snapshot of an external collection database where only the Bee (Apoidea:Anthophila) records are included.

collection.collectionName: USGS Native Bee Inventory and Monitoring Lab
collection.additionalInfo: USGS Native Bee Inventory and Monitoring Lab (USGS PWRC-USGS PWRC)

abstract.para: This collection is a snapshot of an external collection database where only the Bee (Apoidea:Anthophila) records are included.

collection.collectionName: Bee Biology and Systematics Laboratory, USDA-ARS Pollinating Insect-Biology, Management, Systematics Research
collection.additionalInfo: Bee Biology and Systematics Laboratory, USDA-ARS Pollinating Insect-Biology, Management, Systematics Research (USDA-ARS-USDA-ARS)

abstract.para:
This is a one-time snapshot collection of data originally hosted by GBIF: http://www.gbif.org/publisher/1e26a630-7203-11dc-a0d8-b8a03c50a862

Citation: USDA-ARS Bee Biology and Systematics Laboratory. Bee Biology and Systematics Laboratory. Occurrence Dataset https://doi.org/10.15468/anyror accessed via GBIF.org on 2018-02-28.

Temporal range: August 7, 1819 - August 25, 2012

Publication date: November 26, 2019

The Pollinating Insects -- Biology, Management and Systematics Research Unit (PIRU) was founded in the late 1940s as part of the alfalfa seed production unit. We focus primarily on pollination research with bees.The mission of the Pollinating Insect - Biology, Management and Systematics Research Unit is the development of non-Apis bees, for example the alfalfa leafcutting bee and blue orchard bee, as crop pollinators. Research emphasis areas include the development and improvement of management systems for bee populations, biological studies of bees, plant-pollination systems, and bee biosystematics. Cross-pollinated crops not effectively pollinated by honey bees have been targeted for improved pollination management, and the candidacy of selected pollinator species continues to be evaluated. Current research on established species, like the alfalfa leafcutting bee and blue orchard bee, is directed toward developing control programs for pests and diseases, improving management that will result in better bee health and demonstrating pollination efficacy and increased producer profitability on "new" crops.

collection.collectionName: iNaturalist Research-Grade Observations
collection.additionalInfo: iNaturalist Research-Grade Observations (iNat-iNat)

abstract.para:
This is a snapshot collection of Research-Grade observations from the iNaturalist.org platform as published through GBIF.

 

The dataset was searched for the seven families within Anthophila and is available here:

GBIF.org (03 November 2021) GBIF Occurrence Download  https://doi.org/10.15468/dl.aw9qu4

collection.collectionName: Smithsonian Institution, National Museum of Natural History, Entomology Collection
collection.additionalInfo: Smithsonian Institution, National Museum of Natural History, Entomology Collection (NMNH-NMNH)

abstract.para: This collection contains the Anthophila records published by the NMNH Entomology Collection.

collection.collectionName: Collaborative databasing of North American bee collections within a global informatics network project
collection.additionalInfo: Collaborative databasing of North American bee collections within a global informatics network project (AMNH-AMNH)

abstract.para:
Data in this collection are from the Collaborative databasing of North American bee collections within a global informatics network project. Data was originally captured using Arthropod Easy Capture software developed at the American Museum of Natural History (AMNH), New York. Project lead investigators are John Ascher (Principal Investigator) and Jerome Rozen (Co-Principal Investigator) at the AMNH, and Douglas Yanega (Principal Investigator), University of California Riverside. 

Please use this citation for this archive: John Ascher, Digital Bee Collections Network data archive from the Collaborative databasing of North American bee collections within a global informatics network project. Version: 08 Mar 2016. National Science Foundation grants DBI 0956388 and DBI 0956340

collection.collectionName: California Academy of Sciences - Type Collection
collection.additionalInfo: California Academy of Sciences - Type Collection (CAS-CAS)

abstract.para: This collection contains the bee type specimens housed at the California Academy of Sciences.

collection.collectionName: RL Minckley Insect Collection
collection.additionalInfo: RL Minckley Insect Collection (UA-UA)

abstract.para: Insect collection of RL Minckley which is deposited at the University of Arizona. An associated collection of plants is also deposited with the University. A large number of the included insect records are related to a specific vouchered plant.

collection.collectionName: University of Arizona Insect Collection
collection.additionalInfo: University of Arizona Insect Collection (UA-UA)

abstract.para: The University of Arizona Insect Collection (UAIC) contains approximately 2 million specimens representing 35,000 species of pinned, alcohol-preserved, slide-mounted and frozen-tissue preserved arthropods mostly from the Sonoran Desert Region. Significant holdings include Coleoptera, Hemiptera, Hymenoptera, Orthoptera and Lepidoptera, but all insect groups are extensively represented. The UAIC user community is diverse, ranging from outstanding undergraduate and graduate students to highly active emeritus insect systematists. Annually, we host thousands of visitors, ranging from resident researchers to individual walk-ins, campus tour groups, and vast numbers of people seeking information on the biology of the Sonoran Desert Region.

collection.collectionName: San Diego County Bee Checklist
collection.additionalInfo: Regents of the University of California

abstract.para: This is a collection of bees collected in San Diego County, California. All bees are pinned specimens currently stored at the University of California San Diego in David Holway's lab and will be vouchered at the San Diego Natural History Museum.

collection.collectionName: Bee Fauna of National Wildlife Refuges in the Pacific Northwest, 2010-2016
collection.additionalInfo: USDA-ARS Pollinating Insect-Biology, Management, Systematics Research

abstract.para:
In 2010, the Pacific Region of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (Service) initiated a native bee sampling program to document the bee fauna at 15 National Wildlife Refuges (NWR), 1 National Monument, and 1 Bureau of Land Management site in Washington, Oregon, and Idaho (hereafter referred to as Refuges or Sites). This program was comprised of six sampling components, utilizing two methodologies depending on the available staff, site objectives, and accessibility. For purposes of data publication, these components are summarized as follows. [Description continued in original data source.]

Refer to original data source for most updated description and contacts.

collection.collectionName: Wild Bees of Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument
collection.additionalInfo: USDA-ARS Pollinating Insect-Biology, Management, Systematics Research

abstract.para:
We conducted a large-scale, multi-year inventory of the bee fauna of one such area, Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument (GSENM), which is a large area of protected land that, until recently, incorporated nearly 1.9 million acres of the Colorado Plateau in south-central Utah.

Refer to original data source for most updated description and contacts.

collection.collectionName: Barcode of Life
collection.additionalInfo: Barcode of Life (BOLD-BOLD)

abstract.para: Dataset compiled from the Barcode of Life Data Portal (BOLD): Ratnasingham, Sujeevan, and Paul D N Hebert. “bold: The Barcode of Life Data System (http://www.barcodinglife.org).” Molecular ecology notes vol. 7,3 (2007): 355-364. doi:10.1111/j.1471-8286.2007.01678.x

collection.collectionName: J. B. Wallis / R. E. Roughley Museum of Entomology
collection.additionalInfo: J. B. Wallis / R. E. Roughley Museum of Entomology (WRME-WRME)

abstract.para: Invertebrate collection, primarily insects (pinned, slides, and fluid preserved) with good representation for Manitoba, Canada and strengths in bees and aquatic beetles

collection.collectionName: North Carolina State University Insect Collection
collection.additionalInfo: North Carolina State University Insect Collection (NCSU-NCSU)

abstract.para: Housed in the Department of Entomology and Plant Pathology and the College of Agriculture and Life Science, the NCSU Insect Museum is a research collection dedicated to the acquisition and preservation of resources in systematic entomology useful to the NCSU Department of Entomology, the citizens of North Carolina, and the broader systematics community. The collection houses approximately 1.8 million specimens.

collection.collectionName: Oregon State University Hermiston Agricultural Research and Extension Center
collection.additionalInfo: Various

abstract.para: A collection of data for various insect surveys conducted by Oregon State University Hermiston Agricultural Research and Extension Center.

collection.collectionName: Jha Lab - UT Austin - Insect Collection
collection.additionalInfo: Members of the Jha Lab at UT Austin

abstract.para: Insect specimens in this collection were collected for research projects and publications conducted by members of the Jha Lab at the University of Texas at Austin. Specimens have largely been collected in and around the state of Texas but include records from the surrounding region. These records are limited to dried and pinned invertebrate specimens and have been identified and curated by members and collaborators of the Jha Lab.

collection.collectionName: Malheur National Forest Native Bee Collection
collection.additionalInfo: Oregon State University

abstract.para: This collection of pinned bee specimens is the culmination of a Master's thesis conducted at Oregon State University by graduate student Ashley Mertens. This research was conducted under Dr. Jim Rivers of the Forest Animal Ecology Lab within the College of Forestry investigating how prescribed fire and mechanical thinning impact native bee communities in the Malheur National Forest of eastern Oregon. Bees were collected between May-August of 2024 and 2025 using a combination of hand netting, pan traps, and blue vane traps.

collection.collectionName: Steens Mountain Native Bee Collection
collection.additionalInfo: Oregon State University

abstract.para: Database containing taxonomic identifications of native bee specimens collected in the Steens Mountain Cooperative Management and Protected Area between May-August of 2024 and 2025. Specimens were collected via passive and active sampling methods to support research associated with a Master's thesis conducted by Lauren Gramberg and advised under Dr. Jim Rivers of the Forest Animal Ecology Lab within the Oregon State University College of Forestry.

collection.collectionName: UCSD Holway Lab - Longterm Habitat Fragmentation Study
collection.additionalInfo: UCSD Holway Lab - Longterm Habitat Fragmentation Study (UCSD-UCSD)

abstract.para: This is a multi-year dataset collected by Keng-Lou James Hung and Jessica Mullins in San Diego County as a part of research in Dr. David Holway's lab between 2011 and 2024. These projects assess various aspects of how land-use type and climate change impact native bee assemblages

collection.collectionName: Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México - Colección Nacional de Insectos - Abejas
collection.additionalInfo: Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México - Colección Nacional de Insectos - Abejas (IBUNAM-IBUNAM)

abstract.para:
Records of bees deposited in Colección Nacional de Insectos UNAM. 

GBIF.org (23 November 2025) GBIF Occurrence Download https://doi.org/10.15468/dl.rvndjw

collection.collectionName: Bee Regenerative Pollinator Collection
collection.additionalInfo: Bee Regenerative

abstract.para: Bee Regenerative Pollinator Collection consists of specimens collected as a part of projects on working ranches and vineyards across Montana, South Dakota, Oregon, and California. Bee Regenerative is part of the "Bee Girl Organization" which is a women-led nonprofit bridging ecological science and agricultural practice.

collection.collectionName: CalFire Bee Collection
collection.additionalInfo: Oregon State University

abstract.para: Insects collected as part of a study on shaded fuel breaks in northern California.

collection.collectionName: Pine Hill Preserve Area Bee Collection
collection.additionalInfo: Bureau of Land Management - Pine Hill Preserve

abstract.para: Bee collections made by BLM personnel at the Pine Hill Ecological Reserve in El Dorado County, California.
